reasons: Diggie is the cleanest teamwide answer to Atlas's multi-target CC engage.
Time Journey protects nearby allies during the exact window Atlas wants to convert.

proof.summary: Atlas wants to win fights through multi-target CC, while Diggie can remove debuffs and grant nearby allies control immunity during the engage window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves ultimate for Fatal Links.
Diggie positions close enough to protect the caught allies.
failureCases: Atlas baits Time Journey before committing.
Atlas catches allies outside Diggie's protection range.

reasons: Kaja can suppress Atlas before or during the key engage window.
This is more reliable than hook-first logic because Kaja does not need to pull Atlas into grouped teammates first.

proof.summary: Atlas wants to channel Fatal Links after entering, while Kaja's suppression can stop Atlas's action window and drag him away from the ideal teamfight position.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits for Atlas to commit.
Kaja's team bursts Atlas during suppression.
failureCases: Kaja is too far to suppress during Atlas's engage.
Atlas baits Kaja's ultimate before committing Fatal Links.

reasons: Valir can push Atlas away before Atlas reaches the ideal pull position.
His ultimate also gives Valir a self-cleanse and movement window, making him harder to catch personally.

proof.summary: Atlas wants to enter and group enemies, while Valir can knock him back with Searing Torrent before the engage fully connects.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves Searing Torrent for Atlas's real entry.
The team spreads instead of stacking behind Valir.
failureCases: Atlas catches multiple teammates even if Valir escapes.

reasons: Kagura is a strong self-survival answer to Atlas because she can remove debuffs and dash with brief control immunity.
She does not protect the whole team, so her value is personal survival rather than full teamfight denial.

proof.summary: Atlas wants to catch grouped targets with CC, while Kagura can use Rasho Umbrella Flee to remove debuffs, dash, and gain short control immunity.
worksBestWhen: Kagura saves skill 2 for Atlas's real engage.
Kagura avoids standing in the same cluster as teammates.
failureCases: Kagura uses skill 2 before Atlas commits.
Atlas catches teammates instead of Kagura.
Kagura is caught without umbrella position or cooldown.

reasons: Aurora can freeze Atlas before he completes his engage path.
This is a conditional counter because she should not be treated as reliably interrupting Fatal Links after Atlas has already started channeling.

proof.summary: Atlas wants to enter first and start Fatal Links, while Aurora can punish his approach with freeze before the channel begins.
worksBestWhen: Aurora controls the area before Atlas enters.
Atlas is visible before he starts Fatal Links.
failureCases: Atlas starts Fatal Links before Aurora freezes him.
Atlas engages from fog or Flicker angle.
Aurora freezes a low-value target instead of Atlas.
